
From https://www.ljll.fr/~frey/papers/divers/hermite.pdf:

$x_i$ are quadrature points. Lagrange basis is 

$$
\ell_i(x) = \frac{\prod_{k\neq i} (x - x_k)}{\prod_{k\neq i}(x_i-x_k)
$$

```{r}
library(lme4)
gg <-GHrule(3)
xvec <- seq(-3, 3, length = 101)
lagr_basis_i <- function(i, xvec, q) {
  num <- sapply(xvec, \(x) prod(x-q[-i]))
  denom <- prod(q[i]-q[-i])
  num/denom
}
lagr_basis <- function(xvec, q) {
  sapply(seq_along(q), \(i) lagr_basis_i(i, xvec, q))
}
X <- lagr_basis(xvec, gg[,"z"])
par(las=1, bty = "l")
matplot(X, type = "l", col = c(1,2,4), lty = 1)
```

With definitions $H = f(x_i)$.

$$
H(x) = \sum_{i=1}^Q f(x_i) h_i(x) + \sum \frac{df}{dx}(x_i) \bar h_i(x)
$$

$$ 
\begin{split}
h_i(x) & = \left( 1- 2 \frac{d \ell_i}{d x}(x_i) (x-x_i) \right) \ell_i^2(x) \\
\bar h_i(x) &= (x-x_i)\ell_i^2(x)
\end{split}
$$

The integrals of the the $h_i$, $\bar h_i$ functions are $w_i$, $\bar w_i$ (and the $\bar w_i$ terms vanish for the right points).

calculate $h_i(x)$, $\bar h_i(x)$, $df/dx$ by brute force/finite differences?
